firms threatened tile company with |
damage suits and gave his word of |
honor that if the men would return |
to work. he would not hire any men |
for one week. while waiting reply |
from Mr.. Hopper, and If Mr.. Hopper |
hired any men, he. MIR. Aldrich. would |
quite work with u.s.
This was tuesday night, the 12..l, |
and we worked uni. Tuesday noon,
August 10. Mr.. Aldrich said that we
could leave as soon as we pleased.
So We went During the week that
Mr. Aldrich was to hire no men he
hired W. NJ. Atkinson. Jerry Benson
and full crew of men. and as we
passed out one gate they went in
the other.


We turned over the works in good
shape TO Mr. Stout. and Mr.. Stout and
Mr.. Atkinson took our places; we had
| them arrested for running the engine
and boiler without license. They
! pleaded guilty and were fined s25 and
costs.


Now. to be fair with the gas COM
pany and fair with the trades unions
and with the pubic and with our
selves, we, the Gas Makers and Yard
| mems union will pick one man. a
business man. a citizen of Spokane
outside of the trades council or the
trades union, and the gas company
| pick another, a business man outside
of the gas company and citizen of
| Spokane. and submit all of our evil
dence to them and If they find we
are to blame. then we shall disband
as a union If they can not agree,
then a third man may he selected by
the trades council. business man and
a wholly uninterested party. II the
decision is against us. we will ac-
knoledge ourselves beaten and will
give up the fight. and on the other
hand If the decision is against the
gas company then we are to go back
to work as per contract.


JOHN w. CARR,


A. Il, DAVIS.


J. w. WADE.


II. FREEMAN,


L. II. WARREN


When this statement was present
ed to Mr. Hopper this afternoon he
said that he had nothing to say:


You may publish" said he. that
we have no objection to anyone $
coming here to see us upon the CON
troversy, but further than that we
are absolutely silent"
